steps.title: Read two points from the graph
steps.content: The graph passes through $(0,\,0)$ and $(1,\,2)$, indicating the product starts at 0 feet and travels 2 feet per minute.

steps.title: Write the equation
steps.content: The line passes through the origin with slope 2: $d = 2m$. That matches choice A — A is correct.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — 'd = 2m': Slope = 2, y-intercept = 0; matches the graph passing through the origin with a rise of 2 for every 1 unit run. ✓
  • Choice B — 'd = (1/2)m': Slope of 1/2 would mean the product travels only 0.5 feet per minute — inconsistent with the graph's steeper rise. ✗
  • Choice C — 'd = m + 2': A y-intercept of 2 would mean the product starts 2 feet along the belt before any time passes — the graph starts at the origin. ✗
  • Choice D — 'd = 2m + 2': Combines both errors: correct slope but a non-zero y-intercept of 2 that contradicts the graph's origin. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
